title: High CPU Usage
description: Troubleshooting high CPU usage causing slow performance, overheating, or fan noise.

steps.title: Check Task Manager
steps.instruction:
*📊 Step 1: Check Task Manager*
• Press *Ctrl + Shift + Esc* to open Task Manager.
• Click the *CPU* column header to sort by highest usage.
• Identify which process is consuming the most CPU.
_Expected: You can see which app or process is causing high CPU._

steps.title: End High-CPU Processes
steps.instruction:
*🛑 Step 2: End High-CPU Processes*
• In Task Manager, right-click on the process using the most CPU.
• Select *End Task*. If prompted, confirm.
• Do NOT end processes labelled *System*, *lsass.exe*, or *svchost.exe* — these are critical.
_Expected: CPU usage drops after ending unnecessary tasks._

steps.title: Disable Startup Programs
steps.instruction:
*🚫 Step 3: Disable Unnecessary Startup Programs*
• In Task Manager, click the *Startup* tab.
• Right-click any non-essential apps (e.g., Spotify, Skype) and select *Disable*.
• Restart your computer.
_Expected: Fewer programs auto-start, reducing CPU load at boot._

steps.title: Scan for Malware
steps.instruction:
*🛡️ Step 4: Scan for Malware*
• Open *Windows Security* → *Virus & Threat Protection* → Click *Quick Scan*.
• Malware often runs silently and causes high CPU usage.
• If threats are found, click *Remove* and follow the prompts.
_Expected: No threats found, or threats removed._

steps.title: Restart Your Computer
steps.instruction:
*🔄 Step 5: Restart Your Computer*
• Save all your work and restart the computer.
• A fresh restart clears memory leaks and background processes that may be stuck.
• After restart, monitor the CPU in Task Manager to see if it stays normal.
_Expected: CPU usage returns to below 50% after restart._
